text.title: Notion Database Design for Agile PM
text.summary: Projects and Tasks database schemas, relation and rollup field patterns, and formula examples for sprint metrics.

rule.statement: Use a single Tasks database with Sprint as a relation field; never create a new database per sprint.
rule.rationale: Per-sprint databases fragment task history and prevent cross-sprint velocity computation; a single Tasks database with a Sprint relation supports all views and rollups without data migration.

rule.statement: Keep each task as a separate database page, not as a sub-bullet inside a sprint document.
rule.rationale: Only database pages are queryable via the Notion API; sub-bullets cannot be filtered, sorted, or updated by agents.

rule.statement: Limit database properties to 15–20 maximum; archive unused properties rather than accumulating them.
rule.rationale: Complex formulas and many properties slow Notion database loading; unused properties add visual noise and agent confusion.

rule.statement: Tasks database required properties: Task (title), Status (status type — not select), Priority (select: Urgent/High/Medium/Low), Assignee (person), Project (relation), Sprint (relation), Estimate (number), Due Date (date), Tags (multi_select), Blocked By (self-relation).
rule.rationale: Status type (vs select) enables Notion's built-in kanban grouping; self-relation on Blocked By enables dependency tracking without a separate database.

antipattern.description: Using select property type for Status instead of the native status type.
antipattern.reason: Select type loses Notion's built-in grouping behavior (not_started/in_progress/complete) that kanban and board views rely on.

rule.statement: Use bidirectional relations between Tasks and Projects; add rollup properties on Projects for: task count, completed task count, and total estimate sum.
rule.rationale: Bidirectional relations allow navigation both ways; rollups give Projects a live progress indicator without formulas that break when relations change.

example.note: Projects database rollup: "Completed Tasks" — relation: Tasks, property: Status, calculation: Count values, filter: Done. This gives live completion count per project.

rule.statement: Use formulas for derived display fields only (days until due, overdue flag, priority emoji) — never use formulas for metrics that require cross-database aggregation.
rule.rationale: Notion formulas cannot aggregate across databases; cross-database metrics require relation + rollup chains, which are more reliable and auto-update.
